title: Generalized affine Grassmannian slices, truncated shifted Yangians, and Hamiltonian reduction
speaker: Joel Kamnitzer
abstract: Given a representation of a reductive group, Braverman-Finkelberg-Nakajima defined a Poisson variety called the Coulomb branch, using a convolution algebra construction. This variety comes with a natural deformation quantization, called a Coulomb branch algebra. Important cases of these Coulomb branches are (generalized) affine Grassmannian slices, and their quantizations are truncated shifted Yangians. Motivated by the geometric Satake correspondence and the theory of symplectic duality/3d mirror symmetry, we expect a categorical g-action on modules for these truncated shifted Yangians.
tags: Virtual Workshop on Recent Developments in Geometric Representation Theory

proof_stages.label: Dominant affine-Grassmannian slice setup
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines spherical G[[t]]-orbits Gr^lambda, transverse G_1[t^{-1}]-orbits W_mu, and their finite-dimensional intersections W^lambda_mu as affine Grassmannian slices.

proof_stages.label: Structural package for dominant slices
proof_stages.summary: For dominant mu, the slices are presented as conical symplectic singularities with a dimension formula, torus fixed point and MV attracting Lagrangian, an integrable system, Coulomb-branch realization, explicit Yangian quantization, and Satake-theoretic homology meaning.

proof_stages.label: Generalized slices and Yangians for nondominant mu
proof_stages.summary: A loop-group Gauss-decomposition definition extends W^lambda_mu to nondominant mu while retaining the desired Poisson, dimensional, Lagrangian, and quantization features, and the associated truncated shifted Yangians still carry Gelfand-Tsetlin subalgebras.

proof_stages.label: KLRW model for Gelfand-Tsetlin modules
proof_stages.summary: After recalling KLRW diagram algebras, the speaker states the theorem identifying Gelfand-Tsetlin modules for the truncated shifted Yangian with modules over the KLRW algebra, and category O with a standard KLRW quotient.

proof_stages.label: Functorial problem on the Yangian side
proof_stages.summary: Because KLRW module categories have categorification functors, the central problem becomes to describe the corresponding functors intrinsically for truncated shifted Yangians and relate them to geometric Satake.

proof_stages.label: Direct Hamiltonian reduction between adjacent slices
proof_stages.summary: A Hamiltonian G_a-action on W^lambda_mu and multiplication maps yield an isomorphism W^lambda_mu ///_1 G_a = W^lambda_{mu+alpha_i}, with a quantified version, but this direct relation is not compatible enough with Gelfand-Tsetlin subalgebras to construct the desired functors.

proof_stages.label: Coulomb-branch realization of slices
proof_stages.summary: The Braverman-Finkelberg-Nakajima Coulomb branch construction is recalled, and for quiver gauge data extracted from lambda and mu it recovers W^lambda_mu and its quantization by the truncated shifted Yangian.

proof_stages.label: Coulomb-branch parabolic restriction functor
proof_stages.summary: A cocharacter xi of the Coulomb-branch gauge group produces a Levi/invariant Coulomb branch and a Hamiltonian-reduction-like functor, which in the quiver case gives the mu to mu+alpha_i functor and commutes with the known KLRW functor, though its affine-Grassmannian geometry remains unclear.

examples.name: SL_N nilpotent cone
examples.note: Taking G=SL_N, lambda=N times the first fundamental coweight, and mu=0 identifies the slice with the nilpotent cone, where the integrable system, quantization U(sl_N) modulo central character, and usual Gelfand-Tsetlin algebra are familiar.
examples.engagement_reason: he specializes the slice to the SL_N nilpotent cone and identifies its integrable system, quantization, and Gelfand-Tsetlin subalgebra
